sources: FAANG/Big Tech engineering blog hiring posts

smart.label: Technical Depth
what_counts: CS, Math, Statistics, or Engineering major (or equivalent rigor)
Relevant coursework: data structures, algorithms, systems, databases, ML/AI
GPA in technical major (3.5+ for top companies)
Technical certifications: AWS, GCP, Azure, TensorFlow, etc.
Advanced coursework: operating systems, distributed systems, computer architecture
Graduate coursework in CS or related field

grit.label: Shipping and Ownership
what_counts: Quantified impact from internships or jobs (users, latency, revenue, accuracy)
Open source contributions with measurable scope
Personal projects with real users or deployments
Technical leadership: tech lead, team lead, engineering lead

build.label: Technical Portfolio
what_counts: GitHub portfolio with active repos and READMEs
Deployed applications (web, mobile, API) with live links
Internship experience at recognized tech company
Technical blog or writing demonstrating depth

common_gaps.gap: No GitHub portfolio or projects are private
common_gaps.fix: Make your repos public. Add a README to each. Even a class project with clear documentation counts.

common_gaps.gap: Project descriptions lack tech stack and impact
common_gaps.fix: Every project bullet needs: what you built, what tech you used, and what the outcome was (users, performance, accuracy).

common_gaps.gap: No internship experience before senior year
common_gaps.fix: Apply to FAANG new grad pipelines (APM, SWE internship), freshman/sophomore diversity programs (Google STEP, Microsoft Explore).

common_gaps.gap: Resume lists tools without demonstrating how they were used
common_gaps.fix: Replace 'Skills: Python, React, SQL' with project or role bullets that name the tool and the outcome.

common_gaps.gap: No quantified impact in internship or job bullets
common_gaps.fix: Add metrics: reduced latency by X%, served Y users, increased model accuracy from A to B, shipped feature used by N teams.

common_gaps.gap: Only coursework projects, no independent or deployed work
common_gaps.fix: Build one personal project outside of class. Deploy it (Vercel, Heroku, AWS free tier). Even a simple API or web app with a live link is differentiating.

companies.name: Google / Alphabet
companies.hiring_culture: Algorithmic and systems-first. Google values intellectual curiosity and analytical thinking. General Cognitive Ability (GCA) is one of their formal hiring dimensions.
companies.notes: Notorious for bar-raiser culture. One strong 'no' can kill an offer. Prepare for 4-5 rounds. Strong emphasis on communication and the Googleyness dimension.

companies.name: Meta / Facebook
companies.hiring_culture: Move fast, ship often. Meta values speed, impact, and autonomy. Engineers are expected to own full product areas end-to-end.
key_signals: Shipped products and measurable user impact
Ability to work independently on large systems
companies.notes: Strong focus on impact and ownership. Candidates who can demonstrate they shipped something real, scaled it, and owned the outcome stand out.

companies.name: Amazon / AWS
companies.hiring_culture: Leadership Principles-obsessed. Every interview question is mapped to an LP. 'Customer Obsession' and 'Ownership' are the most frequently tested.
key_signals: STAR-format behavioral answers mapped to LPs
companies.notes: Bar raiser process is strict. Behavioral prep is as important as coding here. Research the 16 Leadership Principles and have a story for each.

companies.name: Apple
companies.hiring_culture: Detail-oriented, hardware/software integration, secrecy culture. Apple values craft, polish, and product intuition.
key_signals: Systems and low-level programming for hardware-adjacent roles
Ability to work in a compartmentalized, NDA-heavy environment
companies.notes: Most secretive hiring process in FAANG. Strong portfolio and referrals are the primary pathways. Culture is less process-heavy and more craft-focused than Google/Amazon.

companies.name: Netflix
companies.hiring_culture: High performance, radical candor, no average performers. Netflix pays top-of-market and expects top-of-market output.
key_signals: Senior-level candidates preferred (rarely hires new grads)
Exceptional track record in previous roles
Strong alignment with Netflix culture document
entry_points: Direct application for senior roles; limited new grad pipeline
companies.notes: Rarely hires new grads. Focus on Netflix after 2-3 years of industry experience at another top company.

companies.name: Microsoft
companies.hiring_culture: Growth mindset culture (Satya era). More collaborative than competitive. Values learning, inclusion, and long-term career development.
key_signals: Growth mindset examples in behavioral interviews
companies.notes: One of the most inclusive top-tier hirers. Explore program is a premier pipeline for underrepresented freshman/sophomore CS students.

companies.name: Stripe
companies.hiring_culture: API-first, developer-obsessed. Stripe values writing, clarity of thought, and deep technical ownership.
companies.notes: Known for a writing-heavy interview process. Strong technical writing and ability to explain complex systems clearly is a major differentiator.

companies.name: Jane Street / Citadel / Two Sigma
companies.hiring_culture: Quant-first. Extreme emphasis on mathematical and probabilistic reasoning. Compensation is highest in industry.
companies.notes: CS + Math or Statistics double major is the gold standard pipeline. Most interview processes include probability puzzles, brain teasers, and trading simulations.
